objective: Speakers for an embedded-Rust conference: practitioners who ship Rust on microcontrollers or in firmware and have public speaking or writing evidence
_comment_exclude_people: Optional: people to skip, e.g. already-invited or already-known. Each entry is a name ("Jane Doe") or a profile URL, or {"name": ..., "linkedinUrl": ...}. Also settable via --exclude-file (one per line). Fed to each run's input.exclusion and filtered from results.
rubric_must_haves: Hands-on Rust experience in embedded systems, firmware, or bare-metal contexts (production work or a significant open-source project).
Public evidence of communicating technical work: a conference or meetup talk, substantial technical blog, podcast appearance, or well-documented project.
rubric_signals: Maintainer or significant contributor to an embedded-Rust ecosystem project (embassy, RTIC, probe-rs, an embedded-hal driver).
Shipped Rust firmware in a commercial product.
Prior talks at systems or Rust conferences (RustConf, Oxidize, FOSDEM, embedded meetups).
Writes accessibly about low-level topics for a broad audience.

segments.label: oss_maintainers
segments.focus: Maintainers and top contributors of embedded-Rust ecosystem projects: embassy, RTIC, probe-rs, esp-rs, nrf-hal, stm32-rs, and widely used embedded-hal drivers; check GitHub activity and project docs for who actually does the work.

segments.label: product_firmware
segments.focus: Engineers shipping Rust firmware in commercial products at companies like Espressif, Oxide Computer, Memfault, 1Password (hardware), Sensirion, and embedded consultancies; look at engineering blogs and team pages.

segments.label: conference_circuit
segments.focus: People who have already given talks on embedded Rust or adjacent low-level topics at RustConf, Oxidize, FOSDEM, EuroRust, RustFest, or local Rust meetups; check published schedules and recorded-talk listings.

segments.label: writers_educators
segments.focus: Authors of substantial embedded-Rust writing: the Rust Embedded Working Group book contributors, popular tutorial series authors, course creators, and technical bloggers who cover microcontroller Rust in depth.

_comment_max_per_call: People one discovery run may return per segment. ~12 when each person needs multi-dimension graded research (this config); set null for light-rubric asks like a company roster, which drops the cap so one run returns every person it can verify.
_comment_contact_fields: Empty by default. Set ONLY when the user asked for contact-ready rows. Each entry is a shorthand string ("email", "phone", "uri") or {"key": "workEmail", "format": "email"} for a custom column. Fields are added to the output schema and CSV as nullable values (null when nothing public is found, never fabricated). Contact enrichment costs more per person.
